source.notes: Lead: Head of Product (Sami Ortega) — evaluating Hosted API for 3-week trial; moved to Dedicated sizing after throughput/PTL tests. Hosted trial summary: default gpt-like open weights, 6000 reqs/day, avg tokens per request ~480 (input 160 / output 320). Observed 70% of sessions short QPS (<128 tokens) and 30% long-form (>512 tokens). Latency target: 150-250ms p50 for short chat; will accept 300-450ms for long generations. Cost sensitivity: medium-high — concerned about token spend during nightly batch summarization run. POC results (11/23 demo): 1) prefix caching cut 30% of repeat tokens on FAQ flows; 2) concurrency spikes during morning digest cause tail latency to spike. Requested dedicated sizing worksheet: baseline steady QPS 20, expected business hours peak QPS 85, burst-safe up to 220 QPS for 10-15min windows. Concurrency target: sustain 60 concurrent streams with KV cache warm. Token mix: estimate 65% short (<128), 25% medium (128-512), 10% long (>512). Autoscale preference: mix of steady reserved plus burst buffer (prefer 2:1 spot/burst policy). Routing/fallback: prefer primary high-quality model with automatic fallback to quantized variant on capacity constraints. Security: must support SSO/SAML, enterprise KMS, audit logs and 90-day retention; data residency: prefer US and EU options for regional customers. Quotes: 'need predictable monthly commit, can't accept pay-as-you-go surprises' (Sami). Pricing conversation notes: AE provided draft reserved tiers; looking for a 12-month committed tier with 3-month ramp. Legal ask: standard DPA + SOC2 attestation; procurement wants SLA 99.9% for p50 latency and capacity. Next steps: pricing call 12/04, send final capacity worksheet and cost-per-token bakeoff, legal to receive SOW. POC Jira: RED-3421 open for cache anomalies; engineering asked for token-level telemetry (request/response token histograms).

source.notes:
Company overview: Backstop Commerce is a mid-market e-commerce platform focused on post-purchase customer experience (returns, exchanges, shipping updates) across multiple storefronts. Primary users for Redwood are Product/Engineering (chat agents) and Ops (peak capacity planning).

Sales/Eng summary: evaluating Hosted API first to validate latency SLOs for streaming chat + tool-calling, then considering Dedicated for predictable peak season capacity and data residency. Cost-sensitive but willing to commit if Dedicated demonstrates unit cost improvements under high-concurrency workloads.

Key requirements / success metrics:
- p95 latency target: <= 250ms for streaming partials during normal traffic, <= 350ms absolute cap during peak spikes; overall want consistent p95 across customer sessions.
- Peak season scaling: Black Friday/Cyber Monday projected 4-6x baseline concurrency; need autoscale/burst plan and predictable tail-latency.
- Tool calling: agent must call order API, returns system, and shipment tracking webhooks reliably within flow (sync tool calls <500ms preferred).
- Session affinity / KV cache: reuse KV cache across agent sessions to reduce token costs and latency.
- Observability: token-level latency and per-route p95 dashboards, retention for 6 months for audits.

Recent timeline (CRM-style):
- 2025-10-20: inbound interest (product) via website; AE assigned (Jordan).
- 2025-10-27: discovery call (Jordan + Maya) — high-level requirements, demo request.
- 2025-11-03: hosted API demo showing streaming + tool-calls; internal tech lead joined. Demo notes: streaming partials looked snappy; Redwood demo p95 ~180ms for short-turn utterances in controlled env.
- 2025-11-05: technical deep-dive FF call (ff-20251105-7842) — discussed KV caching, fallback model variants, routing by latency tier.
- 2025-11-12: security questionnaire kickoff (ff-20251112-1120) — legal wants SOC2 + ISO details, KMS options, retention controls. Jira SEC-77 opened for encryption questions.
- 2025-11-15: requested hosted POC plan and pricing ranges (see drive POC deck).

Customer quotes / notes from stakeholders:
- "We can't let p95 spikes exceed 350ms during peak — that drives retries and CS load" — Head of Engineering.
- "If hosted proves reliable under our simulated holiday traffic, Dedicated pricing/commit would be straightforward" — Head of Product.

POC scope (agreed):
- 2-week hosted POC: instrument streaming chat + tool-calling against a synthetic load that simulates 5x holiday peak; capture p50/p95/p99 for token streaming, tool-call latencies, and end-to-end completion time.
- Evaluate fallback behavior: model variant routing (cheaper/quantized) when capacity constrained.
- Validate KV caching impact on average token count and latency.

Open questions / blockers:
- Security: legal/infra need SOC2 + ISO docs and KMS architecture diagram (SEC-77).
- Traffic generation: need Redwood-supplied load script or plan to reproduce Black Friday pattern.
- Pricing: prefer committed Dedicated pricing comparison (monthly commit) vs hosted per-token at projected traffic; need model for break-even.

Next steps (short):
- Redwood: deliver hosted POC plan & load script, final POC dates, and Dedicated pricing ranges (Jordan).
- Backstop: schedule security review slot, provide sample order API endpoints for tool-calling POC, and grant test data access behind VPC if needed.

Linked artifacts: drive://Backstop/Redwood/POC-Plan-v1 (POC steps + load profile), drive://Backstop/Redwood/Security-FAQ (SOC2/ISO answers).

Notes formatting: CRM shorthand, expect additional legal questions after POC.

source.notes:
Summary: CodeSight Labs building next-gen PR review assistant integrated into editors (VSCode, Web IDE). Primary goal: summarize diffs, generate review comments, and rerank candidate suggestions so senior devs see highest-signal feedback first. Wants low-latency streaming for in-editor comment previews and deterministic model/version pinning for reproducible CI checks.

Quoted from CTO: "We need review automation that reduces cycle time without creating noise — deliver concise rationale and one high-quality suggested comment, not 10 weak ones."

Technical requirements / acceptance criteria: - workload: PR-review + batch re-rerank for nightly checks - throughput: expect 1k PRs/day initially, spike to 5k/day in Q2 - latency targets: interactive inline suggestion <= 150ms median, <= 400ms P99 for streaming tokens; batch rerank can be higher (<= 5s) - model preferences: open models with ability to pin exact weights; prefer ~13B-70B options with quantized variants - routing: fallbacks to smaller models during peak; want automatic A/B for canary rollouts - caching: prefix cache for repo-level context to reduce repeated token compute - observability: token-level cost breakdown and per-route latency dashboards - security: must support VPC private control plane, KMS for key management, SSO/SAML, audit logs, and retention controls per EU residency for some customers

POC scope: - 2-week dedicated cluster in Redwood Dedicated (GPU pool) - run 24h low-latency streaming perf test inside dev IDE; measure token latencies and cold-starts - run rerank task on sample of 500 PRs, evaluate precision/recall of top-3 suggestions - integrate SSO and KMS for demo environment - deliver cost model and recommendation from Redwood Optimize (batching/quantization)

Notes/short items (CRM-style): - CTO asked about deterministic rollout and rollback hooks - procurement asked for monthly vs annual pricing scenarios - security: will push SOC2 evidence + legal DPA next week - SE to provide canary config template and suggested quantization profile - "Prefer single-vendor SLA for infra" (CTO)

Stubs/links: drive:/deals/codesight/Redwood-POC-Plan.md, ff_20251103_37xkq, ticket INF-8421.

source.notes:
BluePeak AI (AI-native startup) moved fast: self-serve Hosted API trial, got first streaming chat working quickly, but ran into "what am I allowed to send" / "why am I getting 429" confusion during first load test.

Key notes from evaluation:
- Adoption/TTFS: very strong. They said they were "making successful calls in under an hour" from signup. They used the hosted quickstart and kept streaming on.
- Main friction: quotas + rate limiting. Their team conflates:
  - quota/budget limits (monthly / account-level) vs
  - rate limits (per-minute / per-route) that cause 429s.
  They asked for a single place in Console that says "here is your limit, here is how close you are, and what happens if you exceed it".
- Support-style feedback: they want in-console guidance, not a docs scavenger hunt. Quote paraphrase: "We don’t want to read 5 pages to go live; give us a checklist in the Console and tell us what good looks like."
- Usage dashboard trust: during early testing they thought requests weren’t counted (possible dashboard lag). This created doubt about whether they were under/over quota.
- Streaming: they saw disconnects during a first 'prod-like' test (likely client/network). Asked if Redwood recommends specific retry + reconnect behavior. SE recommended using SDK production defaults + exponential backoff + reconnect helper where available; they requested this be explicitly documented and surfaced in the onboarding flow.

Product asks (aligned with guided onboarding project):
- Add an in-console checklist that includes: API key/env setup, first successful request, streaming established, recommended timeouts/retries, quota/budget configuration, and "interpret a 429" guidance.
- Deep links from the checklist to the exact Console screens (quota/budget, usage dashboard, API key rotation) and to the correct SDK snippet for production defaults.

Commercial:
- Still early-stage; prefers usage-based initially. If they hit consistent >25 RPS and need higher caps, may consider Dedicated later (but not asking now).
- Close depends on confidence in production guardrails + clear cost controls.

Internal follow-ups:
- Put them in the first cohort for Console onboarding checklist feature flag (if eligible).
- Send the production readiness quickstart link + TS/Python snippet with retries/timeouts.
- If they reproduce 429 confusion, point them to Console quota view and clarify distinction between rate limit events and account quotas; ensure they understand any dashboard delay expectations.

Pointers:
- Fireflies call transcript: sources/fireflies/sales-calls/2025-03-04-bluepeak-ai-quickstart-and-first-prod-launch-demo.json
- Related onboarding issues seen elsewhere: 429 surprise (SUP-18510) and usage dashboard lag (SUP-18544) are thematically similar to their feedback (not necessarily their tickets).

- Wants connector model: single API to push batches, real-time upserts, field-level metadata filters, incremental re-indexing.

- Preference: Pinecone-like managed indexes for latency predictability, fallback to Weaviate for hybrid graph/metadata queries; pgvector for on-prem compliance.
- Must support hybrid search: boolean filters + semantic ANN + prefer recent-first recency signals.

- Quote from CTO: "We need predictable tail latency and a clear cost model before committing to dedicated capacity."
- Internal note: procurement window Q2, legal wants data residency clause for EU customers, finance needs unit economics model per 1M queries.

source.notes:
Summary: CareMatrix builds a clinical search layer for enterprise hospital networks. Primary objective: replace brittle keyword search with RAG-based retrieval + reranking over clinical notes (PHI).
Key asks:
1) scale — currently ~120M documents across EHRs (note-level granularity); need sizing guidance and cost modeling.
2) Latency — 95th pctl < 300ms for single-user interactive queries (physician-facing).
3) Cost — target <$0.02 / query after caching and reranking (must show cost calc).
4) Security — HIPAA compliance, strong audit trails, KMS integration, SSO/SAML, per-token access logging and retention controls.
5) Networking — VPC peering + option for on-prem data connector (air-gapped archive ingestion later).

Recent feedback / quotes: "We can't accept any black-box telemetry — every access must be logged and query provenance available for audits" (CISO). "Latency we can tolerate occasional spikes but clinical workflows need sub-300ms for 95p — otherwise clinicians push back" (Head of Product). Cost sensitivity: finance wants TCO model across hosted vs. dedicated vs. private for 3-year horizon.

POC ask: Redwood to ingest a 3-month rolling window (approx 12M clinical notes) into a test index, run both dense embeddings + sparse hybrid retrieval, plus a lightweight reranker. Deliverables: latency & cost benchmarks, ranking quality eval (30 labeled queries, MRR/Recall), security checklist filled, network runbook. Success criteria: 95p latency <300ms, reranking improves top-3 precision by +18% vs. BM25, infra cost within forecasted ARR band.

Operational constraints / shades:
- Data residency: primary copies must remain in-region (NA East).
- Retention: logs must be retained for 7 years (legal requirement).
- Key mgmt: customer-managed KMS mandatory.
- Access model: role-based with break-glass for forensic access.

Open risks: network team needs detailed ports/egress list (blocking VPN peering approval), procurement pushing for committed discount before POC, legal wants stronger indemnity language for PHI. Next steps: share POC runbook + infra cost estimate (Aisha to send 2025-02-22), schedule onboarding call with networking on 2025-02-25.

requirements_summary: Workload: synchronous per-request reranking for auction pipeline (95ms target p95), background embeddings for offline features.
Throughput target: sustained 120k tokens/sec across cluster during business hours, ability to scale to 350k tokens/sec for 60-second bursts during peak auctions.
Concurrency caps: prefer soft concurrency limit per-tenant (200 concurrent requests) with queueing for short spikes; hard limit for safety at 600 concurrent requests.

Burst policy: allow burst beyond reserved tokens/sec for up to 90s, billed separately or charged against burst pool; customer requests burst not to cannibalize reserved throughput metrics used for SLA credits.
Latency SLOs: p50 < 40ms, p95 < 95ms, p99 < 200ms for the reranking path when under reserved capacity.
Model prefs: 7B-13B dense models for latency budget; interested in quantized variants if Redwood can guarantee p95 latency at target throughput.
Routing/fallback: automatic fallback to smaller model family or dedicated low-cost pool if reserved capacity saturated; require predictable downgrade behavior and traceability in Console.
Security: SSO (SAML) integration required, customer-managed KMS for keying, SOC2 compliance evidence and audit log retention for 365 days.
Measurement: want tokens/sec measured at request ingress and also validated by Redwood's console (token accounting) with agreed aggregation window (5-minute and 1-hour rolling averages).
negotiation_points: Minimum tokens/sec definition — customer wants contract language to reference 5-minute rolling sustained tokens/sec vs Redwood prefers 1-hour rolling average for smoothing.
Credit mechanics — customer demands explicit formula for service credits tied to p95 latency breaches and sustained throughput shortfalls; Redwood proposed tiered credits capped monthly.
Burst handling — whether bursts consume reserved capacity quota or are charged separately; customer asks for a burst pool overlay with per-month allotment before metered billing.
Concurrency accounting — discussion whether concurrency caps are per-tenant or cluster-wide; customer wants per-tenant caps to protect multi-tenant performance.
Termination & remediation — customer requested right to terminate or reduce spend if SLA misses exceed 3 consecutive months; Redwood pushing for remediation playbook with canary rollbacks first.
Measurement endpoints — customer wants access to raw telemetry feeds (token counts, queue depth, GPU utilization) for independent verification during audit windows.
Reserved GPU sizing — SE recommended 12x A10G-equivalent GPUs for baseline with burst headroom on shared pool; customer requested pricing for 8, 12, and 16 GPU commit options.
customer_quotes: 'We need predictable throughput during auction windows — a single missed p95 can blow budgets across publishers.'
'If burst eats into our reserved capacity, it's a non-starter — we need a separate burst accounting model.'
'KMS keys must be customer-controlled; we can't accept provider-managed keys for production.'
po_cadence_and_milestones: PO timeline: procurement expects vendor selection by 2025-02, PO sign-off by 2025-03.
PO milestones: initial 3-month ramp with 50% of sustained capacity provisioned, performance validation at month end, full commit activated month 4.
Billing milestone: monthly true-up for metered burst usage; reserved capacity billed monthly with annual commitment discount applied.
technical_notes: SE notes: run load test using Redwood's perf harness with 95th percentile session token distribution; recommend enabling KV cache for repeated prefixes to reduce tokens/sec during hot auctions.
Suggested optimization: quantize candidate model to int8 for reranking path; performance tests to validate p95 latency at 120k tokens/sec.
Monitoring hooks requested: token accounting delta, GPU mem pressure alerts, request queue depth, per-model latency histograms in Console.
next_steps_log: 12/12: AE to circulate draft SLA with 5-minute vs 1-hour measurement options highlighted.
12/13: SE to run two synthetic load profiles (sustained vs burst) and post results to shared Drive.
12/18: Legal exchange: customer to send SLA redlines; Redwood legal to respond within 3 business days.
By 2025-01-10: Agreement on reserved GPU sizing and burst accounting to lock pricing band for procurement.

BYOK process: they need a documented key import path (PKCS#11 or import APIs), key lifecycle operations (rotate, disable, destroy), and rotation windows to be configurable per-environment
Audit/logging: retention controls + signed audit logs required; logs must reference key_id and key_action events; legal wants immutable audit artifacts for 7 years

Data residency: primary model inputs and embeddings must remain in NA region; metadata can be stored in control plane (customer wants control plane in their VPC), asking about optional control-plane isolation
Network: will require VPC peering or AWS PrivateLink; infra team asked for step-by-step peering checklist and required ports; target: non-internet egress for key ops
POC ask: 2-week lab to validate key wrap/unwarp latency, rotate CMK during traffic, and HSM-backed signing of logs. Provide test plan and scripts
Pricing sensitivity: willing to pay premium for Private + HSM features but expects firm uplift numbers; procurement asked for committed pricing for 12-month Dedicated vs Private bundle

notes: Background: mid-market product analytics SaaS; building assistant for product managers + support agents.
Priority: low-latency streaming chat for guided workflows and tool-calling to execute actions (calendar, tickets).
"We need sub-100ms token stream tail for 1k-context typical support flow" — PM quote during 10/18 demo.
Preference: start on Hosted API to iterate quickly; move to Dedicated (reserved) once steady 24/7 throughput is clear.
Security: Okta SSO required (SAML), SOC2 Type II evidence required for procurement, KMS integration desired for key control.
Data handling ask: redact PII from logs (names, emails, credit-card fragments) before stored traces; retention windows: 30d for raw traces, 365d for aggregated metrics.
POC scope: 3-week sandbox — integrate streaming chat, tool-calling hooks, measure p95 latency and cost per 1k tokens. Demo dataset: 1200 sample tickets + synthetic dialog.
Cost sensitivity: mid-market budget; wants model quantization recommendations and batching guidance from Redwood Optimize to hit < $X/active-user mo.
Eng comments: SE ran quick throughput test 10/30; baseline 50 qps with 50ms median compile time; need engine tuning for steady 200 qps peaks.
Sales ask: provide Host vs Dedicated pricing delta and SLA variance clearly in term sheet (see linked drive doc).
Security review status: infra questionnaire sent 10/25; legal team flagged retention + log redaction as must-have prior to procurement.
Quote from CTO: 'We want the fastest path to production but cannot compromise auditability or removal controls.'
Small risk: if Redwood can't show streaming latency guarantees and simple PII-redaction in logs, they'll consider self-hosting evaluation in parallel.

source.notes:
- Initial interest: build IR assistant and internal doc search across incident logs.
- 2024-06-10 intro call: strong exec buy-in (CTO + Head of Product). AO: Jordan Hayes.
- 2024-07-21 demo: showed private deployment flow + latency/cost estimates. Customer asked for on-prem options and KMS integration.
- Security team responded 2024-08: requires full architecture whitepaper, penetration testing plan, and table-top audit before any POC.
- Key quote from Head of Security: "We cannot start a proof-of-concept until we validate offline execution and HSM key control."
- SE (Priya) supplied architecture doc and quantization notes 2024-08-05; security requested longer retention controls and proof of SOC2 evidence.
- Internal champion (Principal ML Engineer, Diego Alvarez) left the company 2024-09; project lost momentum.
- 2024-09-12 finance flagged hiring/budget freeze; procurement deprioritized infra spends until Q2.
- Competitor note: customer ran a pilot with a hyperscaler-managed endpoint (lower friction) during evaluation; leadership preferred turnkey SLA.
- Current status: POC not started — couldn't align on success criteria, security gating and resourcing.
- Suggested re-engage cadence: reconnect in 2025-Q2 after budget cycle; offer a pre-approved security pack and a time-limited evaluation SLA.
- Owner comment: "Good technical fit but organizational timing is the blocker. Will archive and attempt warm re-open later."
